---
description: "Use when writing, reviewing, refining, or preparing LinkedIn posts for this repository. Covers editorial heuristics for technical posts, personal-brand posts, CTA quality, anti-generic writing, and when to recommend text-only versus visual support."
applyTo: "**/.local/linkedin/**"
---
# LinkedIn Editorial Guidelines

- Every post should have one main idea, one practical takeaway, and one conversational CTA.
- Hooks should be specific. Prefer:
  - an unexpected observation
  - a tradeoff or tension
  - a concrete result
  - a short lesson learned
  - a strong opinion with supporting context
- Avoid generic openings that could fit any post.

## Technical Posts

- Lead with the problem, insight, decision, or result.
- Show practical value early.
- Include one concrete example, implementation lesson, or engineering tradeoff.
- Keep jargon under control unless it improves precision.
- CTA should invite discussion, comparison, or shared experience.

## Personal Brand Posts

- Lead with context plus a real lesson.
- Avoid empty celebration. If the post is about a milestone, include what changed, what was learned, or what others can take from it.
- Prefer authenticity and specificity over inspirational tone.
- CTA should invite perspective, not applause.

## Anti-Patterns

- Do not write engagement bait.
- Do not overuse hashtags.
- Do not publish text that sounds like corporate filler or vague motivation.
- Do not repeat the same angle from recent posts when a fresher framing is possible.
- Do not recommend an image if it adds no comprehension value.

## Visual Guidance

- Prefer text-only when the post is strong on story, opinion, or concise insight.
- Prefer a single image when one visual can reinforce the message immediately.
- Prefer carousel when the message depends on sequence, comparison, or multiple steps.
- Avoid text-heavy images for standard feed posts.
